principle: Facets are orthogonal descriptors. Values within a facet may be multi-valued unless exclusive is true.

values.perceptual: Information intended to be perceived as signal or sensation.
values.symbolic: Symbols interpreted through rules and a model.
values.behavioral: Executable, evaluable, interactive, or procedural meaning.
values.stateful: Mutable state, transactions, history, or durable process state.
values.composite: Primarily organizes, packages, or relates other objects.

values.directory: A directory tree is part of the representation.
values.file-set: Multiple coordinated files without a required directory root.
values.byte-stream: A sequential stream, possibly unbounded in operation.
values.block-image: Random-access block address space or device image.
values.memory-image: A serialized process or memory address-space image.

values.text: Character-oriented representation with a declared or inferred charset.
values.mixed: Textual and binary regions are both semantically significant.

values.record-set: Collection of keyed or unkeyed records.
values.table: Rows and columns with a schema or inferred types.
values.relation-set: Multiple relations with keys and constraints.
values.object-graph: Typed objects connected by identity-bearing references.
values.scene: Spatial objects, transforms, geometry, appearance, and cameras.
values.package: Members plus relationships and package rules.
values.filesystem: Names, directories, metadata, links, and allocation structures.
values.opaque-stream-set: Multiple streams whose payload models are separately identified.

values.plain-text: Human-readable character content without required rich structure.
values.structured-text: Text whose syntax expresses a machine-readable structure.
values.audio-signal: Audio waveform, events, or coded audio essence.
values.font: Glyphs, metrics, shaping, and typographic data.
values.tabular-data: Rows, columns, cells, and optional types.
values.relational-database: Relations, constraints, queries, and persistent database state.
values.graph-database: Graph entities, relationships, indexes, and persistent state.
values.geospatial: Coordinates, CRS, features, topology, or rasters tied to Earth/space.
values.cad-bim: Engineering geometry, topology, parametrics, assemblies, or building models.
values.geometry-3d: Meshes, curves, surfaces, solids, materials, rigs, or scenes.
values.archive: Members combined for storage or transport.
values.package: Members plus typed relationships and package semantics.
values.executable: Machine code, bytecode, or loadable executable image.
values.message: A communicative message and its headers/attachments.
values.network-capture: Observed packets, flows, or protocol events.
values.disk-memory-firmware: Low-level block, memory, device, or firmware representation.
values.cryptographic-material: Keys, certificates, signatures, or encrypted envelopes.
values.simulation-model: Executable or declarative model of a dynamic system.
values.domain-record: Professional-domain records requiring a domain ontology.
values.unknown: Semantic family has not yet been established.

values.container: Holds separable payload streams or members.
values.package: Members and typed relationships form the object.
values.compound: Contains subobjects that may not be independently serializable.

values.external-references: Required data may live outside the artifact.
values.hybrid: Some profiles or instances are self-contained and others are not.

values.sampled-continuous: A continuous phenomenon represented by samples.
values.event-stream: Events ordered by logical or wall-clock time.
values.version-history: Historical states are part of the representation.

values.2.5d: Surfaces or elevation over a two-dimensional domain.

values.mutable-state: In-place or transactional mutation is part of normal use.
values.append-only: Normal evolution appends records or segments.
values.executable-state: Execution changes or consumes represented state.

values.streaming: Can be processed with bounded forward-only state.
values.seek-required: Requires non-sequential seek for general processing.
values.random-access: Supports or requires arbitrary element access.
values.whole-object: General processing requires the complete object.
values.index-assisted: An index enables bounded access to subsets.

values.none-known: No active content is defined by the known profile.
values.external-action: Can trigger network, process, or external-resource actions.
values.unknown: Active-content status has not been established.

values.medical: Clinical, imaging, laboratory, and health records.
